questions.question: What is the purpose of a full subtractor in digital design?
questions.explanation: A full subtractor is used to perform subtraction by borrowing from a higher bit

questions.question: What is the difference between a half subtractor and a full subtractor?
options: A half subtractor has two inputs
A full subtractor has two inputs
A full subtractor has three inputs
A full subtractor has four inputs
questions.explanation: A half subtractor has two inputs (A and B) and a carry-out, while a full subtractor has three inputs (A, B, and Carry-in) and a carry-out

questions.question: Which of the following is a correct representation of a half subtractor?
questions.explanation: A half subtractor is represented by a 2-input gate (A and B) with a carry-out

questions.question: What happens to the borrow-out of a full subtractor?
options: It is propagated to the next bit
It is used to create a new borrow-in
questions.explanation: The borrow-out of a full subtractor is lost and cannot be propagated to the next bit

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a use of a half subtractor?
questions.explanation: Half subtractors are used in division to subtract the dividend from the divisor
